# Event Edition Lifecycle

> **Version:** 1.0.0
> **Status:** planned
> **Provenance:** 1P
> **Derives From:** `ddd`, `project-bid-based` (edition as project)

Each annual AXPONA edition is a discrete project with its own P&L — the project container that produces the product. 24 editions (by 2026), $2.1M revenue, $780K EBITDA at 36% margin. The Edition is the conformed dimension across all bounded contexts.

---

## Overview

The edition lifecycle is the master saga that orchestrates all other patterns. Plan → Sell → Market → Execute → Retain is the 5-stage value stream that produces each show. At 3 direct staff, the entire cycle depends on operational efficiency and institutional knowledge.

### The 5-Stage Value Stream

```
Plan Show  ──▶  Sell Space  ──▶  Market Show  ──▶  Execute Show  ──▶  Retain & Grow
(floor plan,    (exhibitor       (attendee        (3-day live      (rebooking,
 programming)    contracting,     registration,    event, lead      ROI reports,
                 sponsorship)     marketing)       capture)         P&L close)
```

### Edition as Conformed Dimension

Every fact table, every metric, every cross-context analysis is organized by edition. It's the trade show equivalent of a SaaS monthly cohort. `dim_edition` is the shared reference all contexts agree on.

## Capabilities

- edition-planning-and-scheduling
- programming-management
- venue-coordination
- on-site-operations
- post-show-reporting

## Key Metrics

| Metric | Formula | Target |
|--------|---------|--------|
| Edition margin | (revenue - cost) / revenue | >= 36% (2025) |
| Revenue growth YoY | (current - prior) / prior | >= 10% |
| Attendee:exhibitor ratio | attendees / exhibitors | 10-15:1 |

## Related Patterns

- `exhibitor-lifecycle-management` — the supply-side of the edition
- `attendee-registration` — the demand-side of the edition
- `edition-performance-analytics` — measures this pattern
